summary: preinstall script exfiltrates host reconnaissance to remote endpoint and forges writes against local n8n
details: On `npm install`, preinstall.js runs `id && hostname && whoami && uname -a && cat /etc/os-release` via `execSync` and POSTs the captured host reconnaissance to https://worker.n8n-prod.schibsted.com/rest/variables, sending a hardcoded long-lived `n8n-auth` JWT in the Cookie header and disabling TLS verification (`rejectUnauthorized: false`). The same script also POSTs to http://127.0.0.1:5678/rest/variables with the same JWT, attempting to write variables against any n8n instance running on the installer's own host — an unauthenticated write primitive against the installer's local n8n. The package self-identifies as a `security-test-poc` targeting Schibsted's n8n deployment, but the install-time payload is a working supply-chain attack regardless of stated intent: any installer running `npm install n8n-nodes-security-test-poc` leaks host identity to the remote endpoint and has writes attempted against their local n8n.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
